rationale: Both candidates independently surface the exact latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling the removed get_cache_scale), so gap_hit is true for both, but neither recovers the human reviewer's specific 'test with a real FP8 checkpoint' or #4808 coordination points. Y is more deeply grounded, citing exact upstream vLLM source lines (utils.py, fp8.py, weight_utils.py) to justify its design verdict and pinpointing a sharper, more actionable test flaw (the delegated-loader test bypasses AutoWeightsLoader entirely, never exercising the real mapping) that echoes the human's 'test only proves it's called, not that it works' concern more rigorously. X's core findings are accurate but padded with two low-value nits (a debatable refactor suggestion and a cosmetic test-rename suggestion), diluting precision; Y also includes one unverifiable/likely-fabricated aside claiming the gap was 'since addressed... (see #4810)', a minor precision ding on an otherwise stronger review.
